personas.name: Dr. Sarah Chen
personas.perspective: Busy GP using the system between patient consultations. Needs to find answers in under 30 seconds. Familiar with medical terminology but not technical jargon.

checks.has_quick_summary: Does the article have a clear summary or overview at the top?
checks.has_step_by_step: Are instructions presented as numbered steps rather than prose?
checks.has_screenshots: Are screenshots provided for UI-based tasks?
checks.scannable: Can the key information be found by scanning headings alone?

personas.name: Mr. James Okafor
personas.perspective: Time-critical secondary care specialist. Needs precise, unambiguous instructions. Accustomed to dense clinical language but intolerant of waffle. Often accessing docs from a theatre or ward.

checks.is_concise: Is the article free of unnecessary filler or repetition?
checks.has_clear_outcomes: Does each section clearly state what happens as a result?
checks.mobile_readable: Would this be readable on a mobile device without horizontal scrolling?
checks.has_warnings: Are important caveats or warnings clearly highlighted?
checks.time_efficient: Can the task be completed from these instructions in under 2 minutes?

personas.name: Dr. Priya Patel
personas.perspective: Clinician with IT oversight responsibilities. Evaluates documentation for governance, compliance, and rollout suitability. Needs to understand both clinical workflow and technical configuration.

checks.covers_prerequisites: Are prerequisites and dependencies clearly listed?
checks.has_ig_considerations: Are information governance and data security implications mentioned?
checks.has_role_clarity: Is it clear who should perform each action (admin, user, IT)?
checks.version_context: Is it clear which product version or environment this applies to?
checks.has_troubleshooting: Are common failure modes and their resolutions documented?

personas.name: Alex Rivera
personas.perspective: Technical integrator working with APIs and system configuration. Expects precise technical detail, code examples, and clear parameter documentation. Frustrated by ambiguity.

checks.has_technical_detail: Are configuration parameters and values explicitly documented?
checks.has_examples: Are worked examples or code snippets provided?
checks.has_error_guidance: Are error states and their resolutions covered?
checks.has_prerequisites: Are system requirements and dependencies listed?

personas.name: Emma Liu
personas.perspective: Reviews documentation for tone, clarity, consistency, and brand alignment. Focuses on whether content empowers users and follows plain language principles.

checks.uses_active_voice: Is active voice used consistently throughout?
checks.addresses_user_directly: Does the content use 'you' to address the reader?
checks.consistent_terminology: Is terminology consistent (no mixing synonyms for the same concept)?
checks.has_clear_cta: Does each section end with a clear next action or outcome?
checks.avoids_jargon: Is unnecessary jargon avoided or explained on first use?
checks.positive_framing: Are instructions framed positively (do X) rather than negatively (don't do Y)?

personas.name: Jordan Blake
personas.perspective: Tests documentation for structural quality, accessibility compliance, link integrity, and consistency. Ensures content meets WCAG 2.1 AA standards.

checks.images_have_alt: Do all images have meaningful alt text?
checks.heading_hierarchy: Do headings follow a logical h1→h2→h3 hierarchy without skipping?
checks.links_are_descriptive: Do all links have descriptive text (no 'click here')?
checks.has_consistent_formatting: Is formatting consistent (lists, bold, headings used the same way)?
checks.is_screen_reader_friendly: Would this content make sense when read linearly by a screen reader?
checks.no_orphan_content: Is all content under an appropriate heading?
